preparation_method: filled with butter and seaweed pork floss
allergens: eggs (likely in dough and salad dressing)

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately one piece (about 80 g), as sold at a Singapore Chinese pastry shop for SGD 3.60.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Doughnuts, yeast-leavened, glazed, enriched (includes honey buns); adapted for butter-fried Chinese dough twist with sugar coating and pork floss filling
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Saa Jung butter-fried dough twist exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. USDA FoodData Central entry for yeast-leavened glazed doughnut (FDC ID 172757) used as the closest structural analogue: enriched wheat flour dough, deep-fried, sugar-coated. Values adjusted upward for fat and sodium to account for butter filling and seaweed pork floss. Energy estimated at ~380 kcal/100 g; protein ~6 g; fat ~18 g; carbohydrate ~48 g. These are best-estimate values; a laboratory analysis of the specific product is recommended for precision.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Doughnut (yeast-leavened, fried) GI = 76 (glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Saa Jung butter-fried dough twist. GI of 76 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for yeast-leavened fried doughnut (glucose reference = 100), which is the closest structurally analogous food: enriched wheat flour dough, deep-fried, sugar-coated. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (76 × 38.4) / 100 ≈ 29.2, where carbohydrate per 80 g serving = 48.0 × 0.80 = 38.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~320 mg/100 g) from butter, seaweed pork floss, and salad dressing
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a piece or fewer occasions per week, as the combined sodium from pork floss, butter, and salad dressing contributes meaningfully to daily sodium targets (WHO recommends <2000 mg/day).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 76) and high refined carbohydrate load (~48 g/100 g) with added sugar coating
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this item as an occasional indulgence only; the high GI (76) and glycemic load (~29 per serving) will cause a rapid postprandial blood glucose rise, and the item should not be consumed without pairing with a low-GI food or protein source to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~7.5 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.3 g/100 g from frying), and high energy density (~380 kcal/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of this deep-fried, butter-filled pastry, as its saturated fat and trans fat content contribute to LDL cholesterol elevation; if consumed, a single small piece on rare occasions is advisable within an otherwise heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Butter-Fried Saa Jung Twist (沙翁) is a Chinese-origin deep-fried pastry sold in Singapore, prepared using the sponge dough method to achieve a characteristically soft, airy interior. The dough is made from wheat flour, milk, caster sugar, and butter, then twisted into a rope shape and deep-fried until golden. The exterior is coated with caster sugar for a crispy, sweet crust, while the interior is filled with a salty butter mixture and seaweed pork floss, creating a distinctive sweet-salty flavour contrast. The homemade salad dressing adds moisture and richness to the filling. Nutritionally, this item is energy-dense (approximately 380 kcal per 100 g), high in refined carbohydrates (48 g/100 g) and fat (18 g/100 g, including ~7.5 g saturated fat), and carries a high glycemic index (GI ~76), making it a high-concern food for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. Sodium content (~320 mg/100 g) is moderate to high due to the pork floss and butter filling. This pastry is best enjoyed as an occasional treat and is not suitable for daily consumption within a health-conscious diet. It is sold at RUXU 入续 糖水铺与包 at Funan Mall, Singapore, for SGD 3.60 per piece.

confidence_reason: No direct laboratory analysis or approved-source entry exists for this specific product. Nutrient values are adapted from the USDA FoodData Central yeast-leavened glazed doughnut entry (FDC ID 172757) with manual adjustment for butter filling and seaweed pork floss. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the structural analogy (enriched wheat flour, deep-fried, sugar-coated) is strong and the adjustments are conservative and documented.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central (yeast-leavened glazed doughnut, FDC ID 172757) adapted for butter and seaweed pork floss filling; a laboratory analysis of the specific RUXU product is recommended for precision. 2. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for the adapted analogue. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for the adapted analogue. 4. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for the adapted analogue. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for the adapted analogue. 6. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for the adapted analogue. 7. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for the adapted analogue. 8. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero as fried wheat-flour dough with butter and pork floss contains no meaningful vitamin C source. 9. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (yeast-leavened fried doughnut); no direct GI measurement for Saa Jung exists. 10. This item contains pork floss and is therefore not halal. 11. The food is classified as Chinese cuisine originating from Singapore's Chinese pastry tradition; multilingual names are provided for English, Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean dish requirements.
